description:
Jira: TBD (inherits from parent epic mutagen-wrd)

Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.coverage

Deliverable:
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md and .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md are ALREADY revised by /distill (r4 revised; r13, r14, s11, s12, s13, v6 added in mutation_pipeline; r1 revised; r8, s8, v5 added in coverage). Do NOT re-author these; verify they are on disk via `mix spec.validate`.
- .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md is ALREADY authored by /distill. Do NOT re-author; verify via `mix spec.validate`.
- README.md:
  - Declare `MutagenEx.Application` as the program entrypoint. Name `mix mutagen` (CLI) and `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1` (library callers) as the actual entrypoint surfaces. DO NOT name `MutagenEx.run/2` (that function does not exist — Option B per refined plan Q1).
  - Append one sentence per SA-10: "Only one concurrent MutagenEx mutation cycle per BEAM is supported; concurrent callers are refused with `{:error, :cover_already_running, _}`. See `.spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md`."
  - Delete the `.13`-residual caveat at L121-125 ("re-run if the tail looks wrong").
  - Add one-liner to the "Installation" section recommending `only: [:dev, :test]` deps scope for dep'ing projects (TC-11).
- CHANGELOG.md:
  - Add `.18` entry per architecture §9.2 but with the corrected latency narrative:
    "User-visible cancellation latency per timeout-classified site is dominated by `cancel_grace_ms` (100 ms by default). The supervisor's `:shutdown_timeout` (5_000 ms) bounds only a hypothetical scenario in which the per-site task itself traps `:shutdown`; in practice the task dies on the signal in microseconds and grandchild teardown is asynchronous afterwards."
  - DO NOT include the "5_100 ms worst case" line (it is wrong — refined plan TC-1 / SA-9).

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - `mix spec.validate` green
  - `mix spec.check` green
  - `mix test` green
  - `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - README renders cleanly (manual readability check)
  - CHANGELOG follows Keep-a-Changelog format
  - Every `Advances:` reference in this epic's tickets resolves to an existing `.spec/specs/` file (a /distill post-condition; this stage re-verifies as part of `mix spec.validate`).

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/* (S1-S3 territory)
- test/mutagen_ex/* (S4 territory)
- mix.exs (S1 territory)

Allowed touches:
- README.md
- CHANGELOG.md
- (defensive) .spec/specs/*.spec.md if `mix spec.validate` flags drift from the /distill output — but the expected change set is zero

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not introduce a new module name in README ("library entrypoint" is `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1`, not a new `MutagenEx.run/2`).
- Do not duplicate the rationale prose from `.spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md` in README. One sentence + a link is the SA-10 directive.
- Do not regenerate spec/decision files — those are /distill's output.
- Do not change CHANGELOG entries for `.11`–`.17`. Only append the `.18` entry.

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix spec.validate green
- [ ] mix spec.check green
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] README has no reference to `MutagenEx.run/2` (manual grep)
- [ ] CHANGELOG has no "5_100 ms" string (manual grep)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0.jsonl

Commit: 3d8b82f
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-docs-and-validation
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.18.5/docs-and-validation (off epic/mutagen-wrd at a3007fc)

Files touched:
- README.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Tests added: none — this stage is docs-only. Verification stubs that this
ticket's two Advances subjects rely on (mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v6 +
mutagen.coverage.v5, both running test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs)
were already flipped to execute: true by mutagen-wrd.18.4 and remain green
under this commit. mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v1-v5 and mutagen.coverage.v1-v4
are all execute: true and green under this commit as part of the project
test sweep.

Verification stubs flipped this stage: none (the relevant ones flipped in
.18.4). Re-verified all spec-verification stubs in both subjects are
execute: true on disk.

Spec/decision validation:
- mix spec.validate / mix spec.check do not exist in this project — there
  is no specled_ex dep. Per .spec/AGENTS.md ("Otherwise, the
  spec-verification block's `command:` and `source_file:` stubs serve as
  the executable contract"), validation is satisfied by every stub in
  mutation_pipeline.spec.md (v1-v6) + coverage.spec.md (v1-v5) being
  execute: true AND passing under mix test.
- Manually re-verified Advances: post-condition: every child of
  mutagen-wrd.18 (.18.1-.18.5) names Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline
  and/or mutagen.coverage; both subjects resolve to existing files at
  .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md and .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md.
- All decision-IDs cross-referenced from both subjects' spec-meta
  resolve to files in .spec/decisions/, including
  mutagen.decision.supervision_tree.

Project verification:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
- mix test: 362 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded)
- mix test test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs: 5 tests, 0 failures
- grep "MutagenEx\.run/2\|MutagenEx\.run(" README.md CHANGELOG.md: no matches
- grep "5_100\|5,100\|5100" README.md CHANGELOG.md: no matches

Scope check:
- Files touched: 2
- Files outside Allowed touches: 0
- Files matching Out of Scope (files): 0

Summary: Documents the .18 supervision-tree work for end users. README
declares MutagenEx.Application as program entrypoint with mix mutagen
(CLI) and MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1 (library) as the actual surfaces
(no fictitious MutagenEx.run/2); appends the SA-10 singleton-cycle
sentence pointing at .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md; recommends
`only: [:dev, :test]` deps scope per TC-11; deletes the obsolete .13
"re-run if the tail looks wrong" caveat and re-numbers the four remaining
gaps. CHANGELOG appends a .18 [Unreleased] entry with the corrected
cancellation-latency narrative (cancel_grace_ms dominates; no "5_100 ms
worst case" string) and the F2-arch partial-closure framing for
hand-rolled trap-exit'd GenServers.

title: S5: README + CHANGELOG + spec/decision validation
